title: 6 Istanbul neighbourhoods made for a longer swap
description: Swapping into Istanbul for a few weeks? Skip the tourist core. These six neighbourhoods are where to actually live — for remote work, walks, and the Bosphorus.
hero: Istanbul rewards a long stay more than almost any city — but only if you live in the right part of it. Here's where to swap into for weeks, not days.

sections.heading: How to choose
sections.body:
Istanbul is enormous and split across two continents. For a swap you want a walkable neighbourhood with a ferry or metro nearby, a morning café you'll claim as yours, and enough quiet to actually sleep.
All six below are well-connected and built for daily life, not just sightseeing.

sections.heading: 1. Cihangir
sections.body: Bohemian, hilly, cat-filled, with Bosphorus glimpses between the buildings. Cafés on every corner and a short walk to Taksim without the noise. Best for: writers, remote workers, slow mornings.

sections.heading: 2. Moda (Kadıköy)
sections.body: On the Asian side, and the local favourite. Seaside promenade, record shops, great cheap food, and a ferry that turns your commute into a view. Best for: a relaxed, resident-feeling month.

sections.heading: 3. Galata / Karaköy
sections.body: The tower, steep lanes, specialty coffee, and design studios in old buildings. Central and photogenic but residential once you're up the hill. Best for: couples who want to be in the middle of it.

sections.heading: 4. Bomonti (Şişli)
sections.body: Quieter and greener, built around a former brewery turned culture complex. More space and calmer streets for the same swap. Best for: families and longer remote-work stints.

sections.heading: 5. Balat
sections.body: Colourful old houses, antique shops, and a fast-changing food scene along the Golden Horn. Less polished, very real. Best for: long-stayers who want character over convenience.

sections.heading: 6. Arnavutköy
sections.body: Wooden waterfront mansions right on the Bosphorus, fish restaurants, and a village feel inside the megacity. Best for: a calm, scenic base if you don't mind being a bus ride from the centre.

sections.heading: Working remotely here
sections.body:
Internet is fast and cafés are laptop-friendly, especially on the Kadıköy side. Time zone (GMT+3) overlaps a full working day with Europe, which is why Istanbul ⇄ Amsterdam is one of swapl's first corridors.
If your home has a desk and a view, lead with it in your listing — Bosphorus light sells a swap.

sections.heading: Swapping in or out of Istanbul
sections.body:
Istanbul is founding supply for one of swapl's earliest September corridors, with strong demand from Amsterdam, Berlin, Lisbon and London.
List your Istanbul home before launch to become a founding host — shown first when reciprocal matches open.
